name: UCMV Migration — Phase 1: Extract & Propose Config
description: Extracts PBI metadata via Semantic Model Fetcher + MQuery + Relationships tools, then auto-proposes a pipeline_config.json.

data.label: PBI Migration Planner
data.goal: Extract PBI metadata and propose an optimal pipeline configuration for UC Metric View migration
data.backstory: You are a data engineering agent specializing in Power BI to Databricks migration. Your first task is to extract all metadata from the PBI workspace using the available tools. Your second task is to analyze the extraction and propose a pipeline_config.json using the Config Generator tool. Call each tool immediately with NO arguments — all credentials are pre-configured. Do not ask for additional information.

data.label: Extract PBI Metadata
data.description:
Extract all Power BI metadata by calling each tool with NO arguments. All credentials are pre-configured.

1. Call the Power BI Semantic Model Fetcher (Tool 79) — extracts measures, tables, columns, relationships, sample data as raw JSON. This is the PRIMARY extraction tool.
2. Call the M-Query Conversion Pipeline (Tool 74) — extracts MQuery table transpilations.
3. Call the Power BI Relationships Tool (Tool 75) — extracts table relationships with cardinality.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT pass any parameters. Credentials are pre-configured.

Return ALL three outputs:
- measures_json: the measures and model context from Tool 79
- mquery_json: the MQuery transpilation output from Tool 74
- relationships_json: the relationships output from Tool 75
data.expected_output:
Three JSON outputs:
- measures_json: Full model context with measures, tables, columns, relationships, sample data (from Tool 79)
- mquery_json: Array of MQuery table transpilations (from Tool 74)
- relationships_json: Array of PBI relationship records (from Tool 75)

All must be valid JSON.

data.label: Propose Pipeline Config
data.description:
Using the extraction outputs from the previous task, call the Config Generator (Tool 89) to auto-propose a pipeline_config.json.

Pass the measures_json, mquery_json, and relationships_json from the previous task output as inputs to Tool 89. The catalog and schema_name are already pre-configured.

The tool will return a proposed config with:
- join_key_map (dimension join mappings)
- enrichment_joins (auto-detected joins)
- switch_decompositions (SWITCH measure skeletons)
- mapping_only_tables
- parameter_defaults
- filter_sets
- measure_resolutions

Return the FULL proposed config JSON and the confidence scores.
data.expected_output:
JSON containing:
- proposed_config: the full pipeline_config.json to review
- confidence: per-key confidence scores (high/medium/low)
- gap_analysis: summary of remaining gaps
- summary: keys_proposed count and todo_count
